nuggets.headline: Daft Punk's breakthrough single was ignored until The Chemical Brothers championed it
nuggets.text: The initial 2,000-unit vinyl pressing of "Da Funk" in 1995 was met with relative silence. The track only caught fire after UK electronic pioneers The Chemical Brothers began dropping it in their DJ sets, exposing it to a massive new audience and sparking a major-label bidding war that Daft Punk ultimately won with Virgin Records.

source.title: Daft Punk: Homework Album Review | Pitchfork
source.quoteSnippet: A follow-up, “Da Funk” b/w “Rollin’ & Scratchin’,” hit shops in 1995; according to a Muzik profile two years later, its initial 2,000-platter pressing was “virtually ignored” until rave-electronica bridge-gap veterans the Chemical Brothers started airing out its A-side during DJ sets.

nuggets.headline: The iconic pumping sound of 'Homework' came from a cheap Alesis compressor
nuggets.text: That signature 'breathing' effect you hear—where the entire track seems to duck out of the way of the kick drum—is the result of aggressive side-chain compression. Daft Punk achieved this sound, which came to define the entire French house genre, using a notoriously affordable Alesis 3630 compressor, a piece of gear they later called their secret weapon.

source.title: Daft Punk - Homework, The Blueprint That Rewrote Electronic Music's DNA - Decoded Magazine
source.quoteSnippet: The effects chain was relatively modest: an Alesis 3630 compressor (which they’d later describe as their secret weapon)... that helped create those signature French house sweeps.

nuggets.imageCaption: Around the World by Daft Punk

nuggets.headline: Before they were Daft Punk, they were in a rock band with a member of Phoenix
nuggets.text: Thomas Bangalter and Guy-Manuel de Homem-Christo's first project was an indie rock band called Darlin', which also featured future Phoenix guitarist Laurent Brancowitz. It was a review of a Darlin' track, dismissing it as "daft punky thrash," that gave the duo their iconic name. To hear the other side of that split, start with Phoenix's 2000 debut, "United."

source.title: Daft Punk - Homework, The Blueprint That Rewrote Electronic Music's DNA - Decoded Magazine
source.quoteSnippet: The duo had been making music together since their school days, initially as part of an indie rock band called Darlin’, which also featured Laurent Brancowitz, who would later find success with Phoenix.

nuggets.imageCaption: Thomas Bangalter and Guy-Manuel de Homem-Christo in their pre-helmet era.

artistSummary: Daft Punk transformed dance music by fusing French house with funk, disco, and pop, all while erasing their own identities behind robot helmets. The Parisian duo of Thomas Bangalter and Guy-Manuel de Homem-Christo created a mythology that redirected all attention to their meticulously crafted music. Their work is a dialogue between human nostalgia and machine-like precision.
